tensions.challenger_profile: T1 challenger: regulation (MCP spec, cannot challenge) vs U4 challenger: stakeholder (negotiable target)

propagation_effects.note: Stub OIDC path for dev/eval lets T1 remain spec-compliant in prod without blocking first-run.
15-min target achievable on stub path; real-OIDC graduation is its own time budget.
Stub path acts as a de-facto break-glass for dev environments; real break-glass (S4) covers prod IdP outage.
First-30-day onboard easier when stub is the eval path.

validation_criteria: `make run` boots with PHRONESIS_OIDC_MODE=stub by default; documented in README
Setting PHRONESIS_OIDC_ISSUER=<real-url> swaps to real OAuth 2.1 + PKCE without code changes
Stub mode is loud: emits a slog warning at startup mirroring the webfs dev-stub pattern

tensions.challenger_profile: B1 challenger: stakeholder vs O1 challenger: stakeholder — both negotiable; O1's threshold is the lever

propagation_effects.note: Per-call audit at B3 scale (1000 service principals) requires the indexed (workspace_id, ts) lookup be fast enough to keep audit-list pages responsive.
90-day raw is the comfortable middle ground; aggregates handle long-window queries cheaply.
SQLite schema must include the audit table with appropriate indexes; migration story applies.

validation_criteria: Synthetic load: 1000 service principals × 100 calls/day for 90 days → raw audit table ≤5 GB
Compactor runs daily; rows older than retention window are purged after aggregate write
Audit list page renders for the latest 1000 rows in ≤500ms p95 at B3 scale

triz.parameters: must require OIDC AND must work without OIDC

tensions.challenger_profile: T2 challenger: stakeholder (OIDC-canonical was a chosen direction) vs S4 challenger: stakeholder (break-glass is a chosen safety) — segmentation honours both

propagation_effects.note: T2's "OIDC canonical" guarantee must explicitly carve out the break-glass principal type so the segmentation is a feature, not a leak.
Break-glass is an entirely separate auth path — different middleware, different audit severity, different env-gate. Lowest coupling.
Break-glass actions need a distinct audit principal type so they are visually separable in audit views.

validation_criteria: Break-glass auth path lives in its own handler; shares NO middleware with the OIDC auth path
Every break-glass use emits an audit row with severity=high and principal.type=break-glass
Default state (PHRONESIS_BREAKGLASS unset) returns 404 for /admin/break-glass — the path doesn't exist when not configured
Test: with break-glass disabled, attempting /admin/break-glass with any header returns 404, not 401

tensions.challenger_profile: T4 challenger: assumption (perf budget guess, must confirm) vs S5 challenger: regulation (suspension propagation is non-negotiable) — T4 yields if needed

propagation_effects.note: Cache must be event-invalidatable, not just TTL'd — adds a small in-process pub/sub. Microbench in m4 must include invalidation overhead.
Event-driven path achieves sub-second propagation in practice; 30s TTL belt covers worst case well under S5's 60s ceiling.

validation_criteria: Revoke a key → next request using that key MUST receive 401 within 30s p99 (well under S5's 60s)
Test with cache pre-warmed: revoke is observed by all in-process consumers via the event channel without polling
Cache TTL is 30s; entries past TTL re-verify against SQLite
Cache invalidation on Argon2id verify failure (defense in depth: bad key never lingers in cache)
cache_invalidation_subconstraints: Invalidation on validation failure: a request whose key fails Argon2id verify triggers a cache eviction for that key prefix
Credential rotation: rotating a key (delete old + create new) invalidates the old key's cache entry across all caches
Forced invalidation: admin endpoint /admin/cache/flush exists, audit-emitting, for emergency operations

tensions.challenger_profile: T6 challenger: technical-reality (panic isolation is a hard property) vs T3 challenger: stakeholder — share the substrate, isolate the dispatch

propagation_effects.note: Dispatch-level isolation (panic-recover, request budget, metrics) is sufficient; full substrate duplication is unnecessary.
One SQLite, one connection pool — normal pattern.
Shared rate-limit pool with separate per-key buckets — the existing pattern in internal/ratelimit extends naturally.

validation_criteria: MCP and HTTP API share: SQLite connection pool, audit drainer, principal resolver, rate-limit buckets
MCP isolates: panic-recover boundary, request budget, /metrics labels
Test: deliberate panic in an MCP tool handler returns 500 to the MCP client; HTTP /readyz still returns 200; no goroutine leak

tensions.challenger_profile: B1 challenger: stakeholder vs T4 challenger: assumption — async drainer pattern is well-established in this codebase

propagation_effects.note: Audit write is non-blocking (channel send + return); request path stays under budget.
Eventual consistency window for audit; crash before drain = lost rows. Failure cascade addresses this.

validation_criteria: Audit enqueue (channel send) is non-blocking — a full channel does NOT stall the request path; a measurable drop counter is incremented instead
Drainer flushes batches on a tick (default 1s) and on shutdown
Drop counter is exposed via /metrics (audit_drainer_drops_total)

failure_cascade.q: What if the drainer can't keep up with audit volume?
failure_cascade.a: Channel fills → drop counter increments → alerts fire → operator scales workers OR throttles producers. The constraint is upheld in expectation; the drop is visible.

failure_cascade.q: What if the binary crashes before the drainer flushes?
failure_cascade.a: Up to 1 batch interval (default 1s) of audit rows is lost. Mitigation candidate: spillover journal (parallel to internal/journal pattern). Logged as suggested_constraint O4-suggested.

failure_cascade.q: What if disk fills and SQLite write fails?
failure_cascade.a: Drainer logs error and retries with backoff; /readyz fails after N retries; operator intervention. Existing pattern.

tensions.challenger_profile: U3 challenger: stakeholder (admin-only was a Round 2 choice) vs B2 challenger: stakeholder — both negotiable; the request-approve flow honours both

propagation_effects.note: Admin still controls issuance — just via approve clicks instead of per-key form-fills.
Onboard time depends on admin response latency. Surfaces an admin-SLA risk: 90% needs admin response within hours, not days.
Users page must show pending key requests as a top-of-page badge so admins can act without polling.

validation_criteria: User submits key request via /settings/keys/request → row inserted into key_requests with status=pending
Admin sees pending count badge on /admin/users; clicking opens the request list
One-click approve: admin clicks Approve → key is minted (S1 plaintext-once flow) → plaintext is delivered to the requesting user via in-app notification + email (if email configured)
Approval audit row links request_id, admin principal, and resulting key_id

triz.note: Not a contradiction — supportive dependency; T1's bearer tokens are exactly the secrets S2 forbids leaking.

tensions.challenger_profile: T1 challenger: regulation (spec) needs S2 challenger: regulation (compliance) implemented first — build order matters

propagation_effects.note: S2 must cover OAuth bearer tokens, refresh tokens, and PKCE code_verifier on top of API keys.

validation_criteria: S2's redaction layer is implemented and unit-tested BEFORE T1's OAuth handlers ship
Redaction patterns include: Authorization header, ?code=, ?state=, ?token=, refresh_token, code_verifier, phr_live_*
Test: deliberately panic with a bearer token in the request — panic stack trace, error response, and audit row contain none of it

evidence.note: Server-side surface only in Stage 1b; Svelte pages deferred to Stage 1c.
Server-side surface only in Stage 1b; Svelte pages deferred to Stage 1c.

evidence.reason: Stub-mode + break-glass sections added in Stage 1b README pass; visual review completes evidence

evidence.pattern: OIDC adapter is using the HMAC-stub

binding_constraint.reason: Bearer-token redaction is the build-order gate (TN8): T1 (OAuth handlers) cannot ship safely until S2's redaction layer covers Authorization headers, OAuth state/code_verifier/refresh_token, and phr_live_* patterns. It is cross-cutting (every log/audit/error-response writer must route through it), it is not present in the codebase today, and a single missed egress point is a credential-leak incident. Closing it unlocks RT-2 (OAuth), which in turn unlocks RT-1 (audit), RT-3 (principals), and the rest. Single mistake = security incident; hardest to verify completeness.

tension_validation.note: RT-12 explicitly carries the stub-OIDC dev path.
RT-13 carries the segmented break-glass path; default 404 when disabled.
RT-4 carries event-driven invalidation + 30s TTL belt.
RT-11 carries MCP sub-handler panic isolation.
RT-10 carries async drainer with bounded loss-on-crash.
Option C drops the Web UI; the request-approve flow would need a CLI-only equivalent. Surface as REOPENED in C.
RT-6 is the binding constraint; build order respected by all options.

solution_options.label: Stage-gated rollout (recommended)
gaps: intermediate (each stage leaves the next stage's RTs unsatisfied)

solution_options.label: MCP-first, defer admin Web UI to v1.1
gaps: RT-9 (admin Web UI deferred; CLI only in v1)

iterations.result: Discovered 24 constraints across 5 categories via interview + pre-mortem. 6 of 24 sourced from pre-mortem (T5, T7, U4, S4, S5, O1). 2 assumption-tagged constraints (T4, O3) flagged for m4 confirmation. 8 draft required truths seeded for m3.

Found 8 tensions, all resolved. 4 contested resolutions confirmed via AskUserQuestion (TN1 stub-OIDC, TN2 90d+per-day, TN4 event+30s TTL belt, TN7 request-approve flow). 4 segmentation-obvious resolutions auto-applied (TN3, TN5, TN6, TN8). 3 sub-constraints generated by tension resolution (O4-suggested journal, S9-suggested cache flush, B4-suggested admin SLA) and staged for promotion. 3 blocking dependencies recorded for m3.

Derived 14 required truths via backward reasoning from outcome. Binding constraint: RT-6 (bearer-token redaction layer) — TN8 build-order gate, cross-cutting, not in codebase today, single mistake = security incident. 3 solution options generated. Option B (stage-gated rollout) recommended: 3 stages each ending in a coherent state, redaction layer first to honour TN8. Solution-tension validation: 7 of 8 tensions CONFIRMED in all options; TN7 REOPENED only in Option C (Web UI deferred).

Stage 1a (binding-constraint substrate) generated. 10 new files + 4 modified files = 14 artifacts total. Coverage: RT-6 (binding), RT-8 (SQLite + forward-only migrations), RT-13 (break-glass env-gated), RT-12 (stub OIDC warning) — 4 of 6 Stage 1 RTs addressed. Deferred to Stage 1b: RT-5 (OIDC projection layer), RT-9 (admin Web UI). Side-effect: Go floor bumped 1.24.5 -> 1.25.0 to admit modernc.org/sqlite v1.50.0; dist-packaging Makefile comment now stale (recorded as drift candidate). All 20 packages green on go test ./...; gofmt + vet + staticcheck clean. 5 reversibility entries recorded; zero ONE_WAY actions.

m5-verify of Stage 1a generation. 4 of 24 constraints SATISFIED, 6 PARTIAL (substrate present, full impl deferred), 14 DEFERRED-by-design (Stages 1b/2/3). All 4 ran tests (TestPanicMessageWithBearerIsRedacted, TestOpenAppliesMigrationsAndIsIdempotent, TestBreakGlassDisabledByDefault, TestBreakGlassEmitsAuditOnSuccess) VERIFIED. Binding constraint RT-6 reaches TESTED level for the redact package itself; cross-cutting wiring is incremental from here. Tension preservation: 8/8 confirmed (none reopened by Stage 1a). Reversibility: 0 ONE_WAY, 2 REVERSIBLE_WITH_COST, 4 TWO_WAY — all acknowledged. Convergence: IN_PROGRESS (by design — Option B is multi-stage).
iterations.binding_status: PARTIAL (TESTED for the package; cross-cutting wiring incremental)

iterations.result: Stage 1b (OIDC projection + admin Web UI server-side) generated. 6 new files + 3 modified = 9 artifacts. RT-5 (OIDC claim->user projection w/ idempotency, suspended-status preservation, role validation) — TESTED. RT-9 server-side (handleAdminUsers + handleAdminKeys backed by SQLite, request->approve flow with Stage-2 501 stub for approve) — TESTED. RT-12 (README documentation pass for stub-OIDC + break-glass) — closes G4 from Stage 1a verify. Server now opens a SQLite store on startup when StorePath is set; existing tests with empty Config{} unaffected. Deferred to Stage 1c: Svelte admin pages (Users.svelte, Keys.svelte), pending-request badge integration in App.svelte, e2e tests. Deferred to Stage 2: real OIDC->principal handoff (so admin handlers' decided_by_user_id stops being NULL), Argon2id key minting (approve flow). All 20 packages green; gofmt + vet + staticcheck clean. 5 reversibility entries added (one REVERSIBLE_WITH_COST: SQLite store field; all others TWO_WAY); zero ONE_WAY.

m5-verify of Stage 1a + 1b. 6 SATISFIED, 9 PARTIAL, 9 DEFERRED of 24. RT-5 (OIDC projection) reaches TESTED with idempotency + suspended-status preservation + role validation tests green. RT-9 reaches TESTED on the server side; Svelte UI deferred to Stage 1c. RT-12 SATISFIED — README documents stub-OIDC and break-glass; G4 closed. Binding constraint RT-6 still PARTIAL (TESTED for the package; cross-cutting wiring incremental). 8 tensions confirmed; 0 reopened. 11 reversibility actions (0 ONE_WAY, 3 REVERSIBLE_WITH_COST, 8 TWO_WAY). G3 (T4 perf bench) still pending — should land before Stage 2 cache. Convergence: IN_PROGRESS (by design — multi-stage).
iterations.binding_status: PARTIAL (TESTED for the package; cross-cutting incremental)

draft_required_truths.draft_statement: An audit-write path exists for every authenticated request boundary (HTTP, MCP, OAuth callback) and is invoked synchronously enough that crash-before-write is bounded.
An OAuth 2.1 authorization server (PKCE-aware) is embedded or proxied; /authorize, /token, and (optionally) /register endpoints exist.
OIDC claim-set normalization to local user projection has a deterministic, idempotent mapping; same claims => same row.
MCP sub-handler runs with its own panic-recover and migration check; failures are isolated from the main HTTP mux.
A redaction pass exists at every log/audit/error-response writer; bearer tokens, OAuth state, and key plaintext are matched and replaced before egress.
Local break-glass admin credential is loadable without OIDC reachability and is independent of the user/key tables.
A revocation propagation channel notifies in-memory session/key caches within 60s of a DB-side suspension; cache TTL is short enough that even a missed signal heals.
A pinned MCP-spec-version smoke test runs in CI against a known-good Claude Code transport client to detect spec drift.

gap_checklist_compliance.note: Core data path: OIDC login -> session -> HTTP/MCP request -> SQLite key lookup -> capability check -> audit row -> wiki/blob action. Each transition crosses a boundary (network for IdP, fsync for SQLite, etc.). Covered by T1, T2, T3, T6, B1.
Crypto/auth attack surface enumerated: OAuth 2.1 PKCE state replay, key brute-force, JWT alg-confusion, token leakage. Covered by T1, S1, S2, S3, S6.
Unauthenticated paths: /authorize, /metrics (if exposed), /readyz. Resource exhaustion bounded by T5 (10 MB response cap), S6 (per-IP ceiling).
External dep is the OIDC IdP. T8 covers timeout (5s) + JWKS cache (24h) + S4 break-glass for total outage.
MCP tool inputs validated against per-tool JSON schema; OAuth callback params validated against state machine. Specific input-validation sub-constraints staged in suggested_constraints.
Concurrent MCP sessions share key/user cache (sync.RWMutex); SQLite write serialization handled by single-writer queue or BEGIN IMMEDIATE; audit drainer already buffered + async per existing pattern.

suggested_constraints.reason: GAP-14: MCP tool input validation against per-tool JSON schema; malformed input returns 400 with no side effect.
GAP-14 + GAP-09: OAuth callback state token has cryptographic length (>=128 bits), single-use, TTL <=10 min.
Failure cascade from TN6: bound the audit-loss window on crash. Candidate: replay-on-startup spillover journal mirroring internal/journal's pattern. Loss window ≤ 1 drainer tick (default 1s).
GAP-16 cache invalidation: forced invalidation endpoint /admin/cache/flush exists, admin-scoped, audit-emitting, for emergency revocation when event channel is suspect.
Admin-SLA derived from TN7: admin must respond to a key request within 24 business hours for B2's 90%-onboard-<10min target to remain meetable; faster (1 business hour) for the median.

blocking_dependencies.reason: Bearer-token redaction must exist before OAuth handlers can ship safely.
Cache invalidation infrastructure (event channel) must exist before T4's cached-auth budget can be claimed.
Break-glass segmentation must be designed alongside T2 to avoid coupling drift.

artifacts.modification_note: added OIDC stub-mode startup warning mirroring webfs.IsStub() pattern
wired redact.NewSlogHandler as default slog handler
added modernc.org/sqlite v1.50.0 + golang.org/x/crypto v0.50.0; bumped Go floor 1.24.5 -> 1.25.0
added Config.StorePath, Server.store field, sqlite.Open in NewServer, store.Close in Server.Close
wired /api/admin/users[...] and /api/admin/keys[...] routes behind withAuth + withAdmin
added 'Local evaluation (stub OIDC mode)' and 'Break-glass admin' sections; closes G4 from prior verify

side_effects.note: Makefile comment 'module's Go floor at 1.24.5' is now stale (go.mod is 1.25.0). dist-packaging T1 (deterministic builds) is unaffected (platform matrix is the threshold), but the comment should be updated in a follow-up. Suggested: amend dist-packaging in a separate /manifold:m5-verify pass once user-mgmt-mcp Stage 1a lands.

reversibility_log.description: Add internal/redact package + wire as default slog handler in main.go
reversibility_log.rationale: Pure additive; reverting deletes the package and restores main.go's import block.

reversibility_log.cost: Pre-release: free. Post-release: any data already written needs to be exported before removing the schema. Currently nothing writes here; cost is zero today.

reversibility_log.rationale: Pure additive; not yet mounted by the server.

reversibility_log.description: Add OIDC stub-mode startup warning in internal/app/server.go
reversibility_log.rationale: Pure log-line addition; reverting is a one-line removal.

reversibility_log.description: Bump go.mod from go 1.24.5 -> go 1.25.0 (forced by modernc.org/sqlite v1.50.0 floor)
reversibility_log.cost: Reverting requires pinning sqlite to <=v1.34.x which is the last 1.24-compatible series; loses ~6 months of upstream fixes. Side effect: dist-packaging manifold's Makefile comment 'Go floor at 1.24.5' now stale and should be updated in a follow-up.

reversibility_log.description: Add modernc.org/sqlite v1.50.0 + golang.org/x/crypto v0.50.0 as direct deps
reversibility_log.rationale: First production direct deps for phronesis. Removing requires backing out the SQLite + Argon2 layers (which is exactly action steps 2 & 3).
Pure additive; ProjectClaims is not yet wired into the OIDC login path (that lands in Stage 2 alongside cookie-OIDC handoff).
Pure additive; routes mounted but only return data; mutating endpoints (suspend/delete/revoke/deny) operate on rows that don't exist until OIDC projection wires up.

reversibility_log.description: Add Server.store + StorePath config; open SQLite at startup
reversibility_log.cost: Pre-release: free. Once any production deployment writes a database, removing the field requires a data export. Currently zero such deployments.

reversibility_log.rationale: Routes are admin-gated and have no public surface; removing them is a routes.go revert.
